title: Git worktrees only in v1
context: The tool needs to define its scope boundary for parallel isolation. Arbitrary parallel checkouts beyond git worktrees would expand the domain and increase ambiguity.
decision: The tool will explicitly support git worktrees in v1 and will not attempt to support arbitrary parallel checkouts.
alternatives: Support arbitrary parallel checkouts (directories) as a generalized model.
Support both git worktrees and arbitrary directories with a unified abstraction.
rationale: This narrows the domain and reduces ambiguity during initial design and implementation. Git worktrees are a well-defined, well-understood git primitive with clear identity semantics.
consequences: The tool's scope is limited to git worktree isolation scenarios.
Non-git-worktree parallel isolation is not supported in 1.0.
The worktree identity model can be designed around git's own worktree primitives.
reversal_trigger: If a compelling use case for non-git-worktree parallel isolation emerges with sufficient user demand and a clear safety model, this decision can be revisited in a post-1.0 cycle.
